title: Stelis ingramii
intro: Stelis ingramii is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, known for its unique and delicate appearance. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its reported health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis ingramii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stelis ingramii is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Stelis ingramii is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which consists of approximately 30,000 species of orchids worldwide. Stelis ingramii is further classified into the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Epidendreae. The specific epithet 'ingramii' likely refers to a person associated with the discovery or study of this orchid species. As with many orchids, the classification of Stelis ingramii may be subject to revision based on ongoing research and taxonomic refinements.
uses: Stelis ingramii has been used in traditional medicine, primarily in its native regions of Central and South America, where it is believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
